title: Sequence Mask
description:
Given a batch of rows $\text{values} \in \mathbb{R}^{b\times n}$ and a per-row valid length $\text{length} \in \mathbb{Z}^b$, zero out every entry `values[k, t]` whose column index $t$ is at least $\text{length}[k]$ — i.e. mask out the padding tail of each row.

**Shapes**: `values`:[b,n], `length`:[b] -> output:[b,n].
starter_code:
import torch

def sequence_mask(values, length):
    # values: [b, n], length: [b]
    # TODO: zero out values[k, t] where t >= length[k]
    pass
hints: Build a `[b, n]` boolean mask by comparing a row of column indices `arange(n)` against the per-row `length`, broadcasting `length` down a new trailing axis.
Multiplying `values` by that boolean mask zeroes out exactly the padded tail of each row — 1 line, broadcasting only.

solutions.name: Broadcast comparison mask
solutions.explanation: `arange(n)[None, :] < length[:, None]` broadcasts to a `[b, n]` boolean tensor that is true exactly where $t < \text{length}[k]$. Multiplying `values` elementwise by this mask keeps valid entries and zeroes the rest.
